executive_summary: The system enables user registration via a form with required fields including first name, last name, username, phone number, email, password, and confirm password. Email verification is mandatory before the first login. Passwords must be 8-12 characters and include at least one number, one special character, and one alphabet character. The system must reject invalid inputs with meaningful inline error messages, detect duplicate emails, and enforce non-empty required fields. Several specification details remain unresolved, such as email format rules, allowed special characters, phone number format, username uniqueness, and email verification process details.
functional_summary: Users can create an account using an email and password. The registration form must collect: first name, last name, username, phone number, email, password, and confirm password. The system shall require email verification before the first login and provide meaningful inline error messages for invalid inputs.
confirmed_business_rules: Password must be between 8 and 12 characters in length.
Password must contain at least one number, one special character, and one alphabet character.
Account is not considered active until email is verified.
validation_rules: Email format must be valid (exact regex not specified – see open questions).
Password must meet complexity requirements (8-12 chars, include number, special, alphabet).
Required fields (first name, last name, username, phone number, email, password, confirm password) cannot be empty.
Duplicate email address must be detected and rejected with a meaningful error.
System shall provide meaningful inline error messages for invalid inputs (confirmed via Q005 answer).
open_questions: What is the exact email format validation rule (regex, allowed characters, length limits)?
What is the list of allowed special characters in password?
Is the username required to be unique?
What is the phone number format and validation rule (e.g., country code, digits, allowed characters)?
What are the character limits for first name and last name?
What are the details of the email verification process (e.g., link expiration time, resend mechanism, link format)?
Is a password blacklist (e.g., common passwords) required?
What HTTP status codes should be returned for different error scenarios (e.g., duplicate email, validation failures)?
Are there any specific error message formats or examples expected for inline errors?
risks: Email verification process details (expiry, link format) are unclear and may lead to user confusion or technical issues.
Password special character set is not defined; inconsistent implementation may result in security gaps or usability problems.
Lack of email format specification may cause validation inconsistencies across frontend and backend.
Phone number format is not specified; different interpretations may cause data quality issues.
Username uniqueness is not specified; potential duplicate usernames may cause conflicts.
HTTP status codes for error responses are not defined; API behavior may be unpredictable for clients.
Two clarification answers (Q001 and Q002) did not match their respective questions, indicating potential communication gaps; underlying requirements remain unconfirmed.
